content: you lent me a pen today and said thanks with this tiny smile. i've been replaying that moment in my head for three hours. i know nothing will ever happen between us, but today was enough.
deleted all our photos. threw away the mug. unfollowed you everywhere. thought i was doing fine until i walked past that thai place we used to go to and my chest just caved in.

we video called again tonight. you smiled but the connection was bad and i couldn't tell if you were actually happy to see me or just going through the motions. i wanted to ask if you still love me but i was too scared of the answer.

all my friends are getting married and i'm here getting excited about a new flavor of instant noodles. it's not that i don't want someone. it's that starting over with a stranger sounds exhausting. but being sick and alone still sucks.

we text until 3am every night. you call me your favorite person. but your instagram looks like i don't exist. i'm so tired of wondering what we are. but asking might end whatever this is, and i'm not ready to lose it.

we used to tell each other everything. then you moved to new york and i stayed here. the texts got shorter, then monthly, then just birthday messages. saw your post today with a new best friend. she was sitting where i used to sit.

we ate dinner in complete silence tonight. not angry silence, just nothing left to say. he scrolled tiktok, i watched netflix. is this really what marriage becomes after three years? two strangers sharing a bed.
i said we weren't right for each other, you said okay and walked away. i said it hoping you'd fight for us. but you didn't. and now i realize you were probably just waiting for me to say it first.
got four questions wrong on a practice test today and completely broke down. cried in the hallway for ten minutes, then wiped my face and went back to studying. 47 days left. i can't afford to stop.

my parents took the tv out of the living room so i wouldn't get distracted. every night mom brings me soup and leaves without saying anything. i know they're more nervous than i am. the pressure sits on my chest like a brick.

been here a year and my english still sucks. every group discussion i'm the quiet one. my roommate asked if i just don't like talking. i smiled. the truth is i'm terrified of opening my mouth and proving i don't belong here.

my advisor rejected my draft for the third time. the feedback said 'what exactly are you trying to research?' i stared at that sentence for twenty minutes, then ordered a case of energy drinks. not sleeping tonight.

four of us had a farewell dinner. we all got way too drunk. my roommate grabbed me and sobbed saying he'd have no one to game with anymore. i cried too. i know we can't go back. i just didn't want it to end.

sixteenth straight day leaving the office after 11pm. caught my reflection in the elevator mirror and my hairline is definitely receding. my boss says we'll get comp time after the project. nobody believes him anymore.

finally sent the resignation email today. four years at this company. started at nothing, worked my way up. but i've been waking up wanting to cry every morning. don't know what comes next, just know i can't stay.

third round interview today. they asked about my five year plan and i just made up something that sounded good. the truth is i have no idea where i'll be in five years. these questions are exhausting.

nobody leaves at six because nobody else has left. we're all done with our work, just performing diligence. today i decided to be the first one out. felt everyone's eyes on my back as i walked to the elevator. dreading tomorrow.

year three. went from a team of six to just me and my cofounder working from my living room. after payroll last month i had $200 in my account. my mom asked how the business is going. i said great. she doesn't know i burned through my savings.

was writing my weekly report at 10am. by 10:30 i was in a conference room being told the company was restructuring. walked out with a cardboard box and stood in the parking lot for twenty minutes. thirty-three years old and suddenly nothing.

my dad texted me today. he learned how to use tiktok and sent me a cooking video, said he'd make it for me when i come home. i replied 'sounds good' and then cried in the bathroom. haven't been home since last chinese new year.

my aunt tagged me in the family group chat again — her neighbor's son works at a bank, want to meet him? my mom dmed me after saying auntie means well. i'm twenty-six. why do they act like i'm past my expiration date.

my son asked today why everyone else's dad picks them up from school. i didn't know what to say. his father barely visits since the divorce. i try so hard to make it feel like we're enough. but some things you just can't fake.

every time i didn't get a perfect score as a kid my dad called me useless. i'm thirty now and i still flinch when my boss sighs in a meeting. i know he loved me. but some wounds take a lifetime to heal.

my mother-in-law has been staying with us for a month. she rearranged the entire kitchen her way. i told my husband i felt uncomfortable and he said she's just trying to help. she means well. but this house doesn't feel like mine anymore.

spent the whole day cleaning. cooked a whole meal just for myself. took a photo, posted it on instagram with a 'weekend vibes' caption. nobody knows i sat on the couch after and stared at the wall for two hours.

my cat was curled up on my lap purring today and it hit me that she'll only be around for maybe ten more years. she can't talk but she knows every time i'm sad. whenever i cry she bumps her head against me like she's saying it's okay.

made instant noodles tonight, added an egg and a sausage. first bite and suddenly i was eight years old again, sitting in my grandmother's kitchen eating her handmade noodles. she's been gone twelve years. i'd give anything for one more bowl.

took myself to a beach town alone. sat by the water all afternoon. couples around me taking selfies, and i pretended to be absorbed in the view. posted a photo saying 'paradise'. mostly i just wanted to prove i could be happy alone.

3:47am. been lying here for four hours. alarm goes off at seven and the more i think about how tired i'll be the more awake i become. took melatonin, didn't help. my brain is a group chat that won't shut up.

landlord called. rent's going up another five hundred. i said is there any flexibility, he said no, market rate. i said ok. hung up and did the math — after rent and bills i'm saving less than a hundred a month.

fourth time moving since graduation. each time i throw more stuff away. things i couldn't let go of before now feel too heavy to carry. today i finally tossed the scarf my ex gave me. guess i've actually moved on.

my chest has been tight for weeks. got every test at the hospital — all normal. the doctor gently suggested it might be anxiety. i didn't book the therapist. but at night my heartbeat is so loud i can't sleep.

i can't remember the last time i genuinely laughed. friends keep inviting me out and i keep saying i'm tired. i'm not tired. i just can't find the point. the things i used to love feel like nothing now. sometimes just existing is exhausting.

nobody wished me happy birthday today. not because they forgot, but because i've never told anyone when my birthday is. bought myself the smallest cake at the bakery. the cashier said happy birthday as she handed it to me. that was the only one.

twenty-five. my friends are in grad school or climbing career ladders or getting married. i'm at a dead-end job with no idea what i want. every morning i wake up and don't know what i'm supposed to be trying for.

sat in the park today and watched a kid chase pigeons. he fell, got up, kept running. it hit me that maybe life isn't as heavy as i make it. bought an ice cream on the way home. the sunset was the kind of orange you can't photograph.

someone i liked in high school told me i was ugly. i still hear that word in my head every time i look in a mirror. nobody says it anymore but i still can't speak up in groups. some words stay with you forever.

my hands were shaking when i read the test results. the doctor said it's early stage, treatable, but it'll cost a lot and i'll need time off. i sat on the hospital steps and cried because i didn't know how to tell my parents.

my grandmother passed while i was in a meeting. my phone was on silent. by the time i called back my mom said she was already gone. i never got to say goodbye. her number is still in my contacts. i can't bring myself to delete it.

every payday the money is gone before it hits my account. credit cards, loans, borrowing from one to pay another. i've stopped opening the statements. last night i dreamed i was debt free. woke up and nothing had changed.
